titleStmt.title: SII 28.250: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 250. (A.R. No. 404A of 1913.)

p.hi: SrI-Vīra-Kṛishṇarāyar-mahārāyar Śaka 1449, Virōdhi, Siṁha, śu. 11, Sunday, Pushya.
p.note: If the cyclic year Virōdhi is taken as more authentic than the Śaka year, the reading of which is very tentative due to the damaged state of the inscription and pUrvva-paksha is taken to be a mistake for apara-paksha (in the Siṁha month pUrvva-paksha ēkādaśI can never coincide with the asterism pushya), the details given regularly correspond to 1529 A.D., August 29-ba. 11-·60; f.d.n. ·06. In that year the simha month began with ba. 11 and ended with ba. 11. The corresponding Śaka year 1451. L.D.Swamikannu Pillai has presumed that the year is a mistake for Sarvajit and that the month quoted, Siṁha is a mistake for mIna, firmly basing himself on the Śaka date, as read; and then suggested an equivalent (Julian calender (A.R.Ep., 1914, p. 68). However, much the drafter of the record might have been mistaken about the Śaka year he could not have been mistaken about the month, current at the time of drafting.

summary.p: The inscription as available is in two disjointed pieces with some portion in between is missing. The continuation of the second piece is also missing. It seems to be a record of a grant of land to a brahmin with the privilege to appropriate some local miscellaneous levies, nall-erudu, naṟ-paśu, nāṭṭu-[viṉiyōgam] etc. and to enjoy the eight kinds of rights in respect of that land, on the occasion of ēkādaśī, for the merit of the king.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . śrīmaṉ makā maṇṭalecuraṉ pāṣaikku tappuva
kaṇṭaṉe koṇṭanāṭu kuṭātāṉ pūṟuvatekṣiṇa paccima
[utara catucamuttirātipati kecaveṭṭai kāṇṭa Aruḷiya śrīvira
piṟatāpaṉ śrīvirakiṟuṣṇarāyar makārāyar piṟuti virācci
yam paṇṇi Aruḷā niṉṟa cakāttam 1[4 100 10 1]9 Itiṉ mel cel
lā niṉṟa viroti caṅvacarattu ciṅṅanāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu
Ēkāteciyum Ātitta vāramum peṟṟa pūca nakṣattiṟattu nāḷ pā
rattuvāca kottirattu ĀciThe writer wanted to write śva but wrote ci finally.lāyaṉa cūttiṟattu kuravaiceriThe writer wanted to write śva but wrote ci finally.

tulukkaṇa kiṟāmam Oṉṟukku nāṉkellaikku
ṭpaṭṭa nañcai puñcai nattamaṭṭukai māvaṭai maravaṭai nal
lerutu naṟpacu nāṭṭuviṉiyokam maṟṟum Eppeṟpaṭṭa cakala camutā
ya pi ttiyum Uṭpaṭa vamāṉiyam Āka Ekāteci puṇṇi
yakālattile kiṟuṇṇarāya makaārāyaṟku taṉmam Āka ku
ṭuttapaṭiyāle ṉetinikṣepam celapāṣāṇa I[kṣi]ṇṇi
yākāmi citta cāttiyam Eṉṉappaṭṭa Aṣṭapoka tecacuvām
miyaṅka Uṭpaṭa nittiya There are three more unmarked pieces in the bundle which seem to be unconnected with the main record as given above; they read:
I 1. ttau aṭaiya tampirāṉāṟku pūcai tiruppaṇi naṭattitte ••••
2. � ••• kale irukkavum yinta •••• tukku yātoruvar a
II 3. ya ••• paṟṟu āṟṟūr ellaiyil cantiracekara nallūr ••• ••• ••• aṉā[n]tāḻvāṉ eḻuttu
III 1. cāṟukarācar piḷḷai puttirar śrīmatu aṉantāḻvār piḷḷai malāṭākiya ce .
2. ṉṉāta vaḷanāṭṭu •••• ṇa ••• m āṟ •• kkūṟṟuttu āṟṟūr u� •
Digital edition of SII 28.250 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
